acceptance_targets: [chapter_01_award_001] Pass if a read of the revised arrival and assignment material anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:47 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:117 shows at least three non-Mera families introduced through action, conflict, or decision with on-page consequence, and no run longer than two consecutive paragraphs functions as pure observational roll call. Fail if the chapter still pauses to summarize family after family before anyone materially affects the scene.

[chapter_01_craft_001] Pass if, reading the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:29 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:33, a reader can count exactly six Mera travelers and align every spoken name with a visible body in the scene. Fail if the dialogue still produces an extra unattached name or leaves one of the six travelers unnamed.

[chapter_01_dialogue_001] Pass if, in the revised cited exchanges, speaker tags and adjacent descriptive lines could be removed and Bram still reads as analytic, Venn still reads as patriarchal and family-centric, and Deshi still reads as sharp domestic authority. Fail if two of those voices could still swap lines without noticeable loss.

[chapter_01_prose_001] Pass if a close read of the revised chapter finds no more than two total instances of any one of these formulae across the cited material ("the way...", "as though/as if...", "not X but Y", or "of someone who...") and each cited paragraph arrives at its image through a distinct syntactic move. Fail if the repeated molds still dominate the descriptive paragraphs.

[chapter_01_prose_002] Pass if the revised Asha beats make her tactile curiosity legible through distinct actions at the cited lines and the narration does not repeat an abstract explanation of that trait more than once after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:5. Fail if later paragraphs still stop to tell the reader, again, that she experiences the world primarily through touch.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a read of the revised arrival and assignment material anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:47 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:117 shows at least three non-Mera families introduced through action, conflict, or decision with on-page consequence, and no run longer than two consecutive paragraphs functions as pure observational roll call. Fail if the chapter still pauses to summarize family after family before anyone materially affects the scene.
findings.evidence: The chapter repeatedly stops for capsule family summaries instead of letting the ensemble arrive through consequential interaction: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:47; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:51; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:67; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:69; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:71; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:117; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:123; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:127; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:129; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:131.
findings.problem: The ensemble arrives as narrated dossiers more than as live pressures on Suna. For a chapter that needs to launch a seven-family confinement premise, too much opening energy goes to roll-call description, so the novel feels efficiently briefed rather than dramatically underway.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress and re-stage the intake blocks anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:47, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:51, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:67, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:69, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:71, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:117, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:123, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:127, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:129, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:131. Keep only details that create immediate consequence, and convert at least three family introductions into interaction with Suna, Olet, or the space pressure of the shrine so the reader meets them through friction instead of census.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, reading the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:29 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:33, a reader can count exactly six Mera travelers and align every spoken name with a visible body in the scene. Fail if the dialogue still produces an extra unattached name or leaves one of the six travelers unnamed.
findings.evidence: Suna says there are six travelers, then gives seven name entries including an orphaned "Mera" while Olet is matching names to visible faces: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:29; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:33.
findings.problem: The registration math breaks. The scene establishes a six-person Mera traveling party, but the spoken list produces an extra name/item, which contradicts the chapter's own visible-body count and the continuity sheet's six-person Mera group.
findings.rewrite_direction: Repair the registration exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:29 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:33. Either separate the family name from the member list with an explicit prompt from Olet, or remove "Mera" from the spoken list so only the six present travelers are counted and named.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised cited exchanges, speaker tags and adjacent descriptive lines could be removed and Bram still reads as analytic, Venn still reads as patriarchal and family-centric, and Deshi still reads as sharp domestic authority. Fail if two of those voices could still swap lines without noticeable loss.
findings.evidence: The narration tells us Bram and Venn have distinct public voices, but their actual spoken lines stay generic and logistical: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:67; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:83; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:87; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:95; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:111; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:135; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:139.
findings.problem: Voice separation is being carried by narratorial scaffolding more than by the dialogue itself. Bram is described as analytic, Venn as patriarchal, and Deshi as domestically cutting, but in scene they mostly register as people asking standard questions or lodging standard complaints.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the exchanges at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:83, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:95, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:111, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:135, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:139 so Bram argues in premise-to-consequence syntax, Venn speaks in family-unit and authority language, and Deshi's objections land as domestic judgment rather than generic dissatisfaction. Do not rely on the setup description at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:67 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:87 to do the differentiation for you.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a close read of the revised chapter finds no more than two total instances of any one of these formulae across the cited material ("the way...", "as though/as if...", "not X but Y", or "of someone who...") and each cited paragraph arrives at its image through a distinct syntactic move. Fail if the repeated molds still dominate the descriptive paragraphs.
findings.evidence: The chapter keeps returning to the same sentence molds: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:25; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:37; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:63; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:67; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:79; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:141; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:167; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:219.
findings.problem: The prose leans too often on repeatable scaffolds such as "the way...," "as though...," "not X but Y," and "with the [quality] of someone who...." By the fourth or fifth reuse, these are no longer choices; they become a tic that flattens the chapter's rhythm and makes the narration feel machine-regular instead of scene-specific.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the descriptive paragraphs centered at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:25, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:37, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:63, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:67, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:79, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:141, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:167, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:219. Replace at least four of the recurring comparison scaffolds with direct statement, scene-specific image, or Suna-specific diction so the chapter stops defaulting to the same sentence mold.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised Asha beats make her tactile curiosity legible through distinct actions at the cited lines and the narration does not repeat an abstract explanation of that trait more than once after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:5. Fail if later paragraphs still stop to tell the reader, again, that she experiences the world primarily through touch.
findings.evidence: Asha's tactile nature is introduced vividly, then re-explained in near-identical abstract language at multiple later beats: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:5; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:21; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:65; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:179; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:183; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:223.
findings.problem: This reads like over-literal reuse of the style bible's Asha brief rather than fresh observation. Instead of letting the trait accrue force, the narration keeps restating that she touches and reads the world through her hands, which turns a vivid behavior into a repeated author note.
findings.rewrite_direction: Choose one or two explanatory beats only. Keep the physical behavior at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:5 and, if desired, one late-night echo at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:223, then cut or radically vary the abstract glosses at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:21, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:65, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:179, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_01.md:183 so Asha's nature is inferred from behavior rather than restated.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel operates in the register between domestic realism and the uncanny. The manifestations must be rendered matter-of-factly — not with horror-genre dread, not with fantasy-genre wonder, but with the flat strangeness of an ordinary impossible thing. A four-year-old rubbing her ear in an old woman's gesture IS strange; the prose should let it be strange without managing the reader's response. The families' emotional reactions provide intensity; the narrative voice stays grounded. Commit fully to the strangeness without explaining it away and without escalating it into the supernatural spectacular. Similarly, commit to the difficult emotional material without providing moral commentary, narrator-managed redemption arcs, or reassurance that things will be all right. Jin's shame, Suna's hidden fracture, Gitta's calculated lie, Bett's unperformed grief, Venn's rigidity — none of these should be softened to make the characters more sympathetic. Trust the reader to hold complexity. The novel's refusal to explain the manifestations definitively is a creative risk — resist the pressure to resolve the ambiguity in the final chapters. The families act in the face of genuine uncertainty, and so must the reader.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for this novel's difficult material — a child's anaphylactic reaction, a drowned boy, a stolen infant, the shame of spousal abandonment, coerced participation in ritual, the uncanny experience of watching your child develop another person's traits, the sexual and emotional charge between Arash and Bett — is a craft failure. Each of these situations demands physical and emotional specificity. Summary, softening, moral self-reassurance, and narrator-managed emotional distance all constitute euphemism. A throat swelling shut must be rendered as a throat swelling shut. A man whose wife left must carry the specific texture of that shame, not a generic sadness. A woman naming her dead child must feel the name in her mouth. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for most characters. Uncontracted forms are reserved for Olet's institutional voice, Gitta's careful precision, Venn's formal commands, and Fasa's ritual language. Even these characters should contract in casual or private speech. If two characters are in a scene and both speak without contractions, at least one voice is calibrated wrong. When in doubt, contract.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate intensity levels across consecutive chapters. A crisis chapter must be followed by aftermath, reflection, or quiet — never by another crisis at the same pitch. Vary scene count (1-3 scenes per chapter), dialogue density (the meal chapter should be mostly action and sensory detail; the debate chapter should be dialogue-heavy), and the ratio of interiority to external action. Chapters 3, 9, and 11 should feel spacious and observational. Chapters 10, 13, and 14 should feel compressed and relentless. Chapter 16 is a single sustained scene between two people. Chapter 18 is expansive and polyphonic. No two consecutive chapters should share structural shape, dominant sense, or emotional register.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded, precise, mid-register. Neither lyrical nor stripped. The prose earns beauty through specificity rather than ornamentation, and earns force through precision rather than intensity. Allow images to land without explaining them. Avoid both prestige-literary arabesques and genre-thriller bluntness. The goal is the transparency of good realist prose that becomes uncanny only when the material itself is uncanny — the manifestations should be rendered with the same flatness as a meal or a sleeping arrangement, and the flatness should make them stranger.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating among five POV characters by chapter assignment (Suna, Jin, Fasa, Leni, Venn). The narration sits behind one character's eyes per chapter, with full access to their thoughts and perceptions. The POV character's emotional state colors the prose — Suna's chapters are more cataloguing and load-bearing, Jin's more fragmented and physically grounded, Fasa's more rhythmic and pattern-seeking, Leni's more colloquial and observational, Venn's more declarative and hierarchical. The narrator's baseline voice provides continuity across POV shifts without flattening the differences.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable, following the POV character's internal tempo and the scene's intensity. Medium-length sentences are the default. Compress for crisis and impact — shorter sentences during manifestations, confrontations, and moments of physical fear. Elongate for reflection, beauty, and communal warmth. Avoid sustained barrages in either direction: no thriller-staccato runs longer than three sentences, no lyrical elongation that loses the reader's breath. The prose should feel like variable breathing — natural acceleration and deceleration, with occasional held breath at moments of revelation.
